# Authenticated layout: shadcn sidebar + tenant dropdown

## Context (already in the repo)

- **Layout hook**: [`apps/org-next/src/routes/_authed.tsx`](apps/org-next/src/routes/_authed.tsx) currently renders only `<Outlet />`. All authenticated routes inherit this; bootstrap data (`tenants`, `currentTenant`, `user`) comes from the `_authed` loader via `getAppBootstrapData`.
- **Tenant switching**: [`apps/org-next/src/hooks/use-user-session.ts`](apps/org-next/src/hooks/use-user-session.ts) exposes `setSelectedTenantId`, which updates the signed `userSession` cookie and `router.invalidate()` so loaders (including bootstrap) refresh. This is the correct client API — no new server endpoint.
- **shadcn / UI location**: Org-next’s [`components.json`](apps/org-next/components.json) maps `ui` to `@workspace/ui/components`. Sidebar primitives (`SidebarProvider`, `Sidebar`, `SidebarHeader`, `SidebarInset`, `DropdownMenu`, etc.) are implemented and exported from [`packages/ui`](../../packages/ui/src/index.ts) (e.g. [`packages/ui/src/components/ui/sidebar.tsx`](../../packages/ui/src/components/ui/sidebar.tsx)). **Do not duplicate** a second sidebar via `npx shadcn` into `apps/org-next` unless something is missing from `@workspace/ui` (it is not for this task).
- **Theme tokens**: [`apps/org-next/src/styles.css`](apps/org-next/src/styles.css) imports `@workspace/ui/theme.css`, which defines `--sidebar-*` variables — sidebar styling should work without new global CSS.

## Target UX

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  subgraph authedLayout [AuthedLayout]
    SP[SidebarProvider defaultCollapsed false]
    SB[Sidebar]
    SI[SidebarInset]
    SP --> SB
    SP --> SI
    SB --> TH[Tenant dropdown + current tenant]
    SI --> OUT[Outlet]
  end
```

- Sidebar **starts expanded**: `SidebarProvider` uses `defaultCollapsed={false}` (see [`sidebar-context.tsx`](../../packages/ui/src/components/ui/sidebar-context.tsx) — `collapsed === false` means open / full width).
- **Current tenant** is visible on the trigger (name + initials avatar, matching the pattern in [`app-header.tsx`](apps/org-next/src/components/app-header.tsx)).
- **Changing tenant**: `DropdownMenu` listing `tenants` from `getRouteApi("/_authed").useLoaderData()`; on select, `setSelectedTenantId(tenant.id)` from `useUserSession()`. Show a check (or similar) on the active tenant; disable items or show loading while `isPending` is true.
- **Edge case — no tenant yet** (e.g. `/organizations/new` when `currentTenant` is null): show a clear empty state in the trigger (“No organization” / “Create organization”) and either an empty menu, a single link to `/organizations/new`, or disabled state — avoid throwing when `tenants` is empty.

## Implementation steps

1. **New presentational component(s)** under `apps/org-next/src/components/` (kebab-case filenames per [`AGENTS.md`](apps/org-next/AGENTS.md)):
   - e.g. `app-sidebar.tsx` (or `tenant-switcher.tsx` + thin shell) that composes:
     - `Sidebar`, `SidebarHeader`, optional `SidebarContent` / `SidebarFooter`
     - `DropdownMenu`, `DropdownMenuTrigger`, `DropdownMenuContent`, `DropdownMenuItem`, `DropdownMenuLabel`, `DropdownMenuSeparator` from `@workspace/ui`
   - Use `Tenant` from [`apps/org-next/src/types/bootstrap.ts`](apps/org-next/src/types/bootstrap.ts).
   - **Note**: [`SidebarFooter`](../../packages/ui/src/components/ui/sidebar.tsx) embeds a `SidebarTrigger` for collapse — acceptable for “default open” with optional collapse. If product wants **no** collapse control, use a custom bottom `div` instead of `SidebarFooter`, or place user chrome there intentionally.

2. **Wire layout in `_authed`**: Replace the minimal `AuthedLayout` in [`_authed.tsx`](apps/org-next/src/routes/_authed.tsx) with a structure analogous to [`AppShell`](../../packages/ui/src/components/app-shell/app-shell.tsx) but **org-next–specific** (do not import `AppShell` — it is typed for `Account` and heavy navigation):
   - Outer: `SidebarProvider` → `div` with `flex h-screen w-full overflow-hidden` (or `min-h-screen` if you need to match existing pages)
   - `Sidebar` (pick `variant` / `className` consistent with org-next; avoid copying unrelated gradient from AppShell unless desired)
   - `SidebarInset` with `flex flex-1 flex-col overflow-hidden` and a scrollable child wrapping `<Outlet />` so child routes keep working

3. **Remove duplicate chrome from child routes** so the shell is not duplicated:
   - Remove `AppHeader` (and the redundant top-level wrappers if they only existed for the header) from [`_authed/index.tsx`](apps/org-next/src/routes/_authed/index.tsx), [`_authed/campaigns/$campaignId.tsx`](apps/org-next/src/routes/_authed/campaigns/$campaignId.tsx), [`_authed/transactions.tsx`](apps/org-next/src/routes/_authed/transactions.tsx).
   - **Refactor or trim** [`app-header.tsx`](apps/org-next/src/components/app-header.tsx): either delete if unused, or keep only pieces still needed (e.g. if some route passes `left` / `right` — today none do). Move **logo link** and **user avatar** into the sidebar shell if they should remain globally visible (header today shows both; sidebar plan should explicitly place them — typically header area + footer).
   - [`_authed/organizations/new.tsx`](apps/org-next/src/routes/_authed/organizations/new.tsx) has its **own** inline header with logo + avatar — remove it in favor of the shared layout to avoid double logos.

4. **Docs**: Per [`apps/org-next/docs/README.md`](apps/org-next/docs/README.md), add a short bullet under an appropriate section (e.g. “Authenticated shell” or extend “Tenant bootstrap”) pointing to `_authed.tsx` and the tenant dropdown component — **pointers only**, not a prose duplicate of the code.

5. **Manual QA**: Logged-in flow — home, campaign, transactions, organizations list/new — confirm sidebar open by default, tenant list matches API, switching tenants refreshes page context and persists after reload; verify `/organizations/new` with zero tenants still renders sensibly.

## Out of scope (unless you ask later)

- Full nav menu in the sidebar (only tenant + shell scaffolding).
- Replacing `AppShell` in other apps or sharing this shell via `packages/ui`.

# Authenticated layout: shadcn sidebar + tenant dropdown

## Context (already in the repo)

- **Layout hook**: [`apps/org-next/src/routes/_authed.tsx`](apps/org-next/src/routes/_authed.tsx) currently renders only `<Outlet />` and its loader redirects users with no tenant to `/organizations/new` (with an exception for that path). Bootstrap data comes from `getAppBootstrapData`.
- **Tenant switching**: [`apps/org-next/src/hooks/use-user-session.ts`](apps/org-next/src/hooks/use-user-session.ts) exposes `setSelectedTenantId` → `updateUserSession` + `router.invalidate()`.
- **shadcn / UI**: Sidebar and `DropdownMenu` live in `@workspace/ui` (see [`components.json`](apps/org-next/components.json)); do not duplicate primitives into `apps/org-next`.
- **Theme**: [`apps/org-next/src/styles.css`](apps/org-next/src/styles.css) imports `@workspace/ui/theme.css` (`--sidebar-*` tokens).

## Routing model (clarified)

**Goal:** The sidebar is **never mounted** until there is at least one resolvable tenant. Onboarding (no tenant) uses a **separate** layout under `_authed` with **no** sidebar.

```mermaid
flowchart TB
  subgraph authed [/_authed]
    L[Loader: bootstrap + redirects]
    OUT[Outlet]
  end
  subgraph onboarding [Onboarding route no tenant]
    ONB[Plain layout e.g. min-h-screen header optional]
  end
  subgraph sidebarLayout [Pathless e.g. /_authed/_sidebar]
    SP[SidebarProvider defaultCollapsed false]
    SB[Sidebar + tenant dropdown]
    SI[SidebarInset + Outlet]
    SP --> SB
    SP --> SI
  end
  L --> OUT
  OUT --> onboarding
  OUT --> sidebarLayout
```

1. **`_authed.tsx` (parent)**  
   - **Loader**: Keep `getAppBootstrapData()`. If authenticated and **no** `currentTenant` / empty tenant list (match existing product rules), `redirect({ to: "<onboardingPath>" })` **except** when `location.pathname` is already the onboarding route (same pattern as today’s `onOrganizationsNewRoute` check).  
   - **Component**: Only `<Outlet />` — **no** `SidebarProvider` here. Optionally a fragment/wrapper with no chrome so it is obvious this is “auth only.”

2. **Pathless layout (e.g. `_authed/_sidebar.tsx`)** — name should read clearly in the file tree (`_sidebar`, `_main`, `_app-shell`, etc.):  
   - **Loader**: Either inherit parent data only (`parentRoute` / `beforeLoad`) **or** call `getAppBootstrapData` again — TanStack Start patterns already used elsewhere. **Guard**: if `!tenant` (or `tenants.length === 0`), `redirect` to `<onboardingPath>` so direct URL hits cannot mount the sidebar.  
   - **Component**: `SidebarProvider` (`defaultCollapsed={false}`) + flex row + `Sidebar` (tenant dropdown in header/footer) + `SidebarInset` + `<Outlet />` for child routes.

3. **Onboarding route(s)** — **siblings** of the pathless sidebar folder, not nested under `_sidebar`:  
   - e.g. `/_authed/<onboarding>/...` with their own full-page UI (existing inline header on create-org page is fine here).  
   - **Onboarding URL**: You chose a custom path (not `/organizations/new` and not the proposed `/campaigns/new` in the picker). **Replace `<onboardingPath>` in this plan with the exact path you provide in chat** before implementation (may require adding/renaming/moving the current [`organizations/new.tsx`](apps/org-next/src/routes/_authed/organizations/new.tsx) or adding a new route file).

4. **Move routes** that should show the sidebar under the pathless layout’s directory so they inherit the shell, for example:  
   - `index`, `campaigns/$campaignId`, `transactions`, and (if it should show the shell) `organizations/index` → under `_authed/_sidebar/...` with matching URL paths preserved via TanStack route config / folder structure.  
   - Regenerate/update [`routeTree.gen.ts`](apps/org-next/src/routeTree.gen.ts) via the project’s route codegen after moves.

## Sidebar UX (only when `_sidebar` is active)

- **Default open**: `SidebarProvider` with `defaultCollapsed={false}` ([`sidebar-context.tsx`](../../packages/ui/src/components/ui/sidebar-context.tsx)).  
- **Tenant dropdown**: `DropdownMenu` + `tenants` / `currentTenant` from the **sidebar** route’s loader data (update `getRouteApi` to the new path, e.g. `/_authed/_sidebar`).  
- **No “empty tenant” UI in the sidebar** — that state never renders because onboarding is outside this layout.

## Implementation steps (ordered)

1. **Confirm onboarding path** with you (custom URL) and whether existing create-organization UI moves or is linked from the new path.  
2. **Restructure routes**: add pathless `_sidebar` (or chosen name); move tenant-backed pages; leave onboarding outside; fix imports and `createFileRoute` ids.  
3. **Redirect logic**: centralize in `_authed` + optional duplicate guard in `_sidebar` loader.  
4. **Components**: `app-sidebar.tsx` (or similar) with `Sidebar*` + tenant `DropdownMenu`; `setSelectedTenantId` from `useUserSession()`.  
5. **Strip duplicate `AppHeader`** from routes now under `_sidebar`; keep onboarding pages self-contained. Trim/remove [`app-header.tsx`](apps/org-next/src/components/app-header.tsx) if unused.  
6. **Docs**: [`apps/org-next/docs/README.md`](apps/org-next/docs/README.md) — document two layouts, redirect, onboarding path, and key files (per [`AGENTS.md`](apps/org-next/AGENTS.md)).  
7. **QA**: No tenant → always onboarding, no sidebar in DOM; with tenant → sidebar visible; tenant switch persists; deep links to `/_sidebar` routes without tenant redirect.

## Out of scope (unless requested)

- Full primary nav in the sidebar (tenant shell only).  
- Porting [`AppShell`](../../packages/ui/src/components/app-shell/app-shell.tsx) from other apps.

# Authenticated layout: shadcn sidebar + tenant dropdown

## Context (already in the repo)

- **Layout hook**: [`apps/org-next/src/routes/_authed.tsx`](apps/org-next/src/routes/_authed.tsx) currently renders only `<Outlet />` and its loader redirects users with no tenant to `/organizations/new` (with an exception for that path). Bootstrap data comes from `getAppBootstrapData`.
- **Tenant switching**: [`apps/org-next/src/hooks/use-user-session.ts`](apps/org-next/src/hooks/use-user-session.ts) exposes `setSelectedTenantId` → `updateUserSession` + `router.invalidate()`.
- **shadcn / UI**: Sidebar and `DropdownMenu` live in `@workspace/ui` (see [`components.json`](apps/org-next/components.json)).
- **Theme**: [`apps/org-next/src/styles.css`](apps/org-next/src/styles.css) imports `@workspace/ui/theme.css` (`--sidebar-*` tokens).

## Decisions (updated)

- **Single layout file**: Implement the sidebar shell in **`_authed.tsx` only** — **no** pathless `_sidebar` route, **no** moving route files under a new folder, **no** second layout component file under `routes/` unless a presentational extract (e.g. `app-sidebar.tsx`) is needed for clarity.
- **Same shell for all authed routes**: **[`/organizations/new`](apps/org-next/src/routes/_authed/organizations/new.tsx)** uses the **same** `_authed` layout as home, campaigns, transactions, and organizations list — simplifies routing and avoids special-casing that page.
- **Zero tenants in the UI**: The sidebar **always mounts** under `_authed`. If there are **no tenants** (or nothing to select), show a short static message such as **“No tenants yet”** — **no** link to the create flow in the sidebar; navigation to create is handled by the **existing root-level loader redirect** (user hits `/` or other disallowed routes without a tenant → `/organizations/new`).
- **Sidebar contents (v1)**: Tenant switcher **when** `tenants.length > 0`; otherwise the empty copy above. **No** logo, user avatar, or primary nav in the sidebar (deferred).
- **After removing `AppHeader`**: **No** replacement inset header for v1 — main content starts beside the sidebar. Remove the **duplicate inline header** on `organizations/new.tsx` so it matches.
- **Collapse**: Use **`SidebarFooter`** from `@workspace/ui` so collapse/expand remains.

## Routing model (simplified)

```mermaid
flowchart TB
  subgraph authed [/_authed single file]
    L[Loader bootstrap plus redirects]
    SP[SidebarProvider]
    SB[Sidebar tenant UI]
    SI[SidebarInset]
    OUT[Outlet all child routes]
    L --> SP
    SP --> SB
    SP --> SI
    SI --> OUT
  end
```

1. **`_authed.tsx`**
   - **Loader**: Keep `getAppBootstrapData()`. Keep the **existing** redirect: authenticated and **no** resolvable tenant → `redirect({ to: "/organizations/new" })` **except** when `location.pathname === "/organizations/new"` (same idea as today’s `onOrganizationsNewRoute`). Adjust pathname check if the constant changes; no second layout loader.
   - **Component**: `SidebarProvider` (`defaultCollapsed={false}`) + flex row + `Sidebar` (tenant area + `SidebarFooter`) + `SidebarInset` + `<Outlet />`.

2. **Child routes**

Here’s what was going wrong and what we changed.

**Cause:** In `@workspace/ui`, `SidebarFooter` always renders a `SidebarTrigger` with `text-background` (and a small `h-7 w-7` control). On a light sidebar, that usually matches the page background, so the chevrons looked **missing** even though the control was there.

**Change:** Stop using `SidebarFooter` in `app-sidebar.tsx`. Use:

1. **`SidebarContent`** with `min-h-0 flex-1` so the column layout stays correct.  
2. A **bottom bar** with **`SidebarTrigger`** only — the default trigger uses `text-sidebar-foreground`, so the collapse/expand control stays visible.  
3. **`shrink-0`** and **`bg-sidebar`** on **`Sidebar`** so the column doesn’t shrink in the flex row and uses the intended sidebar background.

The toggle should now show clearly at the bottom of the sidebar; behavior is still collapse/expand via the same `SidebarTrigger` component.

input.plan:
# Fix tenant dropdown foreground/background

## Root cause

[`packages/ui/src/components/ui/dropdown-menu.tsx`](packages/ui/src/components/ui/dropdown-menu.tsx) sets `DropdownMenuContent` to:

- `bg-sidebar-background text-sidebar-foreground`

In [`packages/ui/src/styles/theme.css`](packages/ui/src/styles/theme.css) (`:root` light mode), `--sidebar-background` is a **dark** indigo (`oklch(0.256 0.089 281.01)`, commented “indigo-900”) while `--sidebar-foreground` is **dark** slate text (`oklch(0.129...)`). That pairing yields **dark text on a dark panel** (or very poor contrast).

Your sidebar column uses `bg-sidebar` on [`AppSidebar`](apps/org-next/src/components/app-sidebar.tsx), which maps to the **`--sidebar`** token (light `oklch(0.984...)` in `:root`) — a different variable than `sidebar-background`. So the **dropdown portal** looks like a dark sheet while the rest of the shell reads light; text inherits `text-sidebar-foreground` and looks wrong.

`DropdownMenuItem` also uses `focus:bg-sidebar-accent` / `focus:text-sidebar-accent-foreground`, which are consistent with the broken mental model but do not fix default row text.

## Recommended fix (org-next only, minimal blast radius)

**Do not change** the shared package dropdown defaults (other apps may rely on them). **Override** in one place:

1. **`DropdownMenuContent`** in [`apps/org-next/src/components/app-sidebar.tsx`](apps/org-next/src/components/app-sidebar.tsx)  
   Add Tailwind classes so the menu matches standard app surfaces, e.g.  
   `bg-popover text-popover-foreground border-border`  
   (keep existing `min-w-56`, `align="start"`). These tokens are already defined for light/dark in the same theme file (`--popover`, `--popover-foreground`).

2. **`DropdownMenuItem`** (tenant rows)  
   Add base + focus overrides so rows match the popover surface and standard menu affordances, e.g.  
   `text-foreground focus:bg-accent focus:text-accent-foreground`  
   alongside existing `cursor-pointer gap-2`. Rely on `cn` / tailwind-merge in the UI package to resolve conflicting `focus:*` utilities with the defaults from `DropdownMenuItem`.

3. **Optional polish**  
   - Adjust the `Check` icon from `text-primary` to something that reads on popover if needed (often `text-primary` is fine).  
   - Quick visual check in browser: open tenant menu in light layout and confirm hover/focus states.

## Out of scope unless you ask

- Editing [`packages/ui` theme.css](packages/ui/src/styles/theme.css) to “fix” `--sidebar-background` / `--sidebar-foreground` globally (would affect every consumer of those tokens).  
- Changing the shared `dropdown-menu` component in `packages/ui` (broader impact).

## Verification

- Manual: authenticated home with multiple tenants — dropdown panel is light-on-light (or correct dark mode when `.dark` is applied), tenant names readable, hover/focus visible.  
- `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit` and `pnpm exec biome check` on the touched file.
